siderably above that at which it is
now being held.


The drawing at Occur d'Alene
opened this morning with consid-
cradle enthusiasm in spite of the
fact that more numbers have al
ready been drawn than can be sup
piled with land in the Occur d.
Alene reservation. There are but
about 1900 claims there. The only
chance there is for those above
1900 lies in the possible failure of
those below 1900 to make entry on
the land.


Should one of these fail to make
entry, then the first number above
1900 will be notified of the chance
and so on till the lands are ex-
hausted. TIL names being drawn
today from 1900 up to 5000 are to
those below 1900 fail to make their
entry.


Spokane has been the luckiest in
the Occur d'Alene drawing, her
people taking more than all of
Idaho put together. Only few of
the eastern people drew lucky num-
bers, though hundreds of them
crossed the continent to make ap-
plication. The middle west Jared
little better, then came Washing
ton, Oregon and Idaho, with a few
lucky numbers for Californians and
a liberal number for Montana.


OF course little Helen Hamilton,
who drew number one for Isadore
Segil of Myrtle Creek, Ore, is
about the most popular little miss
in the whole world with hundreds
of Uncle Sans lucky people today
and maybe she can't be blamed if
she does watch express packages
for little tokens of gratitude.
w. w. Leeper, before whom Sc
lig registered, wired his lucky eli
ent a neat little suggestion of a
silk hat or maybe little financial
congratulatory remembrance, i'm
mediately after he learned of his
success yesterday.


"You registered before me and
drew No. 1. w. w. Leeper," the
message said, SO why shouldn't
Helen have visions of silk dresses
and the like?
